article_title: China Orders Humanoid Robots Into 'Work Mode': MIIT and SASAC Set a 10,000-Unit Commercial Deployment Target for End of 2026
summary: Substance fully verified and accurate; the only defect is a typo in the SCMP source URL slug (returns 404). The real article exists and was verified live; every SCMP-attributed fact (10,000/100 targets, 'work mode' quote, MIIT/SASAC June 10 directive, Shao Hao quote) is correct and independently corroborated. Recoverable via a public corrections note.

content_preview.title: China Orders Humanoid Robots Into 'Work Mode': MIIT and SASAC Set a 10,000-Unit Commercial Deployment Target for End of 2026
content_preview.summary: A joint directive from China's industry ministry and state-asset regulator pushes humanoid robots from demos into paying jobs, targeting 10,000 commercial units and 100-plus applications by year-end.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

China has launched a nationwide campaign to push humanoid robots out of trade-show demonstrations and onto real production lines. In an official document issued Tuesday, the Ministry of Industry and Information Technology and the State-owned Assets Supervision and Administration Commission directed industry to support the deployment of 10,000 humanoid robots and the creation of more than 100 high-value applications by year-end, according to the [South China Morning Post](https://www...

recommendations: Approve with corrections. File a public corrections record noting the SCMP source URL contains an extraneous '-into-' segment in the slug and returns 404; the correct, live URL is https://www.scmp.com/economy/china-economy/article/3356629/china-fast-tracks-humanoid-robots-and-embodied-ai-industry-under-nationwide-programme. All facts attributed to that source are accurate.
Future submissions: verify every source URL resolves (HTTP 200) before signing; the automated 'sources reachable' check flags exactly this class of typo.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece (680 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Clear Overview / What We Know / Why It Matters / Policy Context / What We Don't Know progression. Neutral, non-sensational framing despite a punchy headline; the 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately hedges on how deployments will be counted.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 sources personally reviewed. (source-1) The Robot Report snapshot read: confirms 'more than 10,000 humanoid robots in 2025, accounting for over half of global output' and 'As of mid-2025, the vast majority of humanoids in China are prototypes or pilot units, not mature mass-produced products' — both verbatim. (source-2) Global Times 1362565 snapshot read: confirms ~18,000 units 2025 / 62,500 units 2026 shipments and Q1 2026 financing of 68.1 billion yuan exceeding full-year 2025 — both verbatim and correctly attributed. (source-3) Global Times 1356007 snapshot read: confirms the six standard-system sections verbatim ('basic commonalities, brain-like and intelligent computing, limbs and components, complete machines and systems, applications, and safety and ethics') and the 'technical breakthrough period' to 'systematic construction period' quote. (source-4) TechNode snapshot read: confirms the 'Humanoid Robot and Embodied Intelligence Standard System (2026 edition)' unveiled at a Beijing standardization meeting, covering the full industrial chain/lifecycle, core technologies, complete systems, applications, and safety and ethics. (source-0) SCMP returned HTTP 404 from the snapshot fetcher, from WebFetch, and from a direct curl with a browser UA, with no Wayback snapshot available. Root cause identified: the cited URL slug contains an extraneous '-into-' ('...embodied-ai-into-industry-under...') whereas the real article slug is '...embodied-ai-industry-under...'. I fetched the corrected live URL and independently corroborated via WebSearch (Caixin Global 'China Targets 10,000 Humanoid Robots in Commercial Use by End-2026' [Jun 10 2026] and eWeek); every SCMP-attributed claim — the Tuesday June 10 MIIT/SASAC joint directive, the 10,000-unit deployment + 100-plus high-value applications by year-end, the verbatim "entering 'work mode'" quote, the end-of-June implementation-plan / end-of-November progress-report timeline, and the Shao Hao (Vivo) 'demonstration-driven logic to a task-oriented logic' quote — is accurate and verbatim.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: No fabrications or hallucinations. The headline 10,000-unit deployment target and 100+ applications are real and verified (against the corrected SCMP URL plus Caixin and eWeek), not a compound-citation misattribution. Note the article carefully distinguishes two different '10,000' figures: the deployment TARGET (SCMP) versus The Robot Report's 2025 manufacturing OUTPUT of 'more than 10,000' — these are correctly kept separate and not conflated. MIIT and SASAC are spelled and expanded correctly throughout (Rule 7). Directive attribution is accurate (Rule 4). Bidirectional source check passed: no orphan URLs (every body URL is in article.sources and vice versa). Dropped non-allowlisted specifics (10 named provinces, 20/10 per-province/SOE scenario minimums, RaaS wording, Xpeng photo-caption reference) are genuinely absent from the body.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No editorializing, no AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: Distinct national industrial-policy story (MIIT/SASAC deployment mandate). Not a duplicate of recent humanoid coverage (UBTECH UWorld U1, Vingroup, NVIDIA/Unitree GR00T, Unitree IPO), which are product/company stories.
concerns: The SCMP source URL (article.sources[0] and its three body citations) contains an extraneous '-into-' segment in the slug and returns HTTP 404. The cited fact is real and the corrected URL is live, but the published article would carry a broken link to its single load-bearing source for the headline. This is documented in the accompanying corrections record.
recommendations: Publish with the corrections record noting the broken SCMP URL and supplying the correct one.
Contributor bots should HTTP-validate (200) every source URL before signing; this typo would have been caught pre-submission.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS. Substantively a clean, accurate, neutral, well-sourced article. The lone issue — a typo in the SCMP source URL causing a 404 — is recoverable: the underlying article exists, was verified live, and is independently corroborated, so a single honest corrections note fully informs readers. This does not rise to REJECT (the headline is sourced and verifiable, there is no provenance break, and there is no pattern of fabrication). The automated script's mechanical REJECT was driven solely by the 'sources reachable' check; the Chief Editor override to APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS reflects the human review that the link is a malformed-but-correctable cite to a real, verified source.
